message:
security: prevent private-key commits — broader .gitignore + .githooks/pre-commit + AGENTS.md

A real Ed25519 signing private key (signing-77c6e768.key) was committed
in feature commit d1342ce despite .gitignore already containing
`signing-*.key`. The leak was force-added (`git add -f`), which bypasses
.gitignore. Cloudflare auto-revoked nothing because Cloudflare doesn't
look at signing keys; nobody noticed until a separate cleanup pass
spotted the file at the workspace root. The .key has now been excised
from history via `git-filter-repo --invert-paths --path
signing-77c6e768.key` and force-pushed (commits 088ab26..7da1ec3, both
main and master branches).

This commit hardens against a recurrence with three layers of defense:

1. .gitignore — broader filename patterns covering generic private-key
   conventions, not just signing-*.key:
   - *.key (with !*.pub.key carve-out for tools that name pubs that way)
   - *.priv, *.pem, *.pfx, *.p12 (PKI bundles)
   - id_rsa, id_dsa, id_ecdsa, id_ed25519 (canonical SSH key names)
   - secrets.*, *-secret, *-secret.* (common secret-bundle conventions)
   These would have caught the leaked file even without the
   signing-specific rule.

2. .githooks/pre-commit — a bash hook that runs on `git commit` and
   inspects the staging area before the commit object is created:
   - Filename matching against the same patterns as above (catches
     `git add -f` bypasses since the file still ends up in the index).
   - Content scan for staged files <= 64 KB looking for:
     * Provider-token shapes: sk-..., ghp_..., gho_..., ghs_...,
       AKIA<16-uppercase-alnum>, xox[abprs]-... (Slack)
     * PEM private-key headers: `-----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY-----`
   Skipped on merge/rebase commits (those don't have a meaningful
   "did you mean to add this" question). Bypass for false positives:
   `git commit --no-verify` (which the hook documents in its error
   message).
   Smoke-tested locally: staging a fake `test-fake.key` and trying
   `git commit` rejects with the expected file-pattern violation.

3. AGENTS.md — adds a one-line setup step for the hook
   (`git config core.hooksPath .githooks`) under the existing
   "Install guards" section, alongside the agent-mail / chain-runner
   guard install instructions.

Why all three layers: (a) .gitignore stops the innocent path, (b) the
pre-commit hook stops the `git add -f` bypass that produced the prior
leak, (c) AGENTS.md ensures cloners know they need to opt into the hook
once. Removing any one layer leaves a real foot-gun open.

Out of scope: pre-receive hooks on the GitHub side (would require a
GitHub Action or third-party scanner like trufflehog/gitleaks). The
project-local layers above catch the vast majority of failure modes
without external dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,95 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Pre-commit guard against accidental secret commits.
+#
+# Activate per-clone with:
+#     git config core.hooksPath .githooks
+#
+# Why this exists: signing-77c6e768.key was committed in a feature commit
+# despite .gitignore having signing-*.key — someone used `git add -f`. This
+# hook is a second layer that refuses the commit at the index level so even
+# `git add -f` can't sneak a private key in.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+red()  { printf '\033[31m%s\033[0m\n' "$*" >&2; }
+yel()  { printf '\033[33m%s\033[0m\n' "$*" >&2; }
+
+# Skip merge commits and rebases
+if [[ -f .git/MERGE_HEAD || -d .git/rebase-merge || -d .git/rebase-apply ]]; then
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+# Files staged for this commit (added/modified/copied/renamed; excludes deletions)
+mapfile -t staged < <(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR)
+
+if [[ ${#staged[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+violations=()
+
+# 1. Filename-based blocklist — common private-key/secret naming conventions.
+#    The patterns below are intentionally conservative: any tracked .pub file
+#    is fine, but a .key (or .priv, or signing-XYZ without an explicit .pub
+#    suffix) is rejected at commit time.
+for path in "${staged[@]}"; do
+    base=$(basename -- "$path")
+    case "$base" in
+        # Private key file extensions
+        *.key|*.priv|*.pem|*.pfx|*.p12)
+            # Allow .pub.key (some tools name pubs with .pub.key)
+            if [[ "$base" != *.pub.key ]]; then
+                violations+=("$path  (matches private-key filename pattern)")
+            fi
+            ;;
+        # SSH private keys (no extension)
+        id_rsa|id_dsa|id_ecdsa|id_ed25519)
+            violations+=("$path  (matches SSH private-key filename)")
+            ;;
+        # Project-specific signing keys — Ed25519 seeds named signing-<hex>
+        signing-*)
+            # Allow only paired .pub files
+            if [[ "$base" != *.pub ]]; then
+                violations+=("$path  (matches signing-* private-key naming convention)")
+            fi
+            ;;
+        # Common secret bundles
+        secrets.*|*-secret|*-secret.*|*.secret|*.secret.*)
+            violations+=("$path  (matches secret-bundle filename pattern)")
+            ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+# 2. Content-based heuristics on small staged files (<=64 KB).
+#    Catches tokens that don't have suspicious filenames.
+for path in "${staged[@]}"; do
+    [[ -f "$path" ]] || continue
+    size=$(wc -c <"$path")
+    [[ "$size" -le 65536 ]] || continue
+
+    blob=$(git show ":$path" 2>/dev/null || true)
+    [[ -n "$blob" ]] || continue
+
+    # Common provider token shapes (kept conservative to avoid noise)
+    if grep -E -q '\b(sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{30,}|gho_[A-Za-z0-9]{30,}|ghs_[A-Za-z0-9]{30,}|AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|xox[abprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{20,})\b' <<<"$blob"; then
+        violations+=("$path  (contains provider-token-shaped string)")
+    fi
+
+    # PEM private-key headers
+    if grep -E -q -- '-----BEGIN (RSA |EC |DSA |OPENSSH |PGP |ENCRYPTED |)PRIVATE KEY' <<<"$blob"; then
+        violations+=("$path  (contains PEM private key header)")
+    fi
+done
+
+if [[ ${#violations[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+    red "✗ Pre-commit blocked: likely secret file(s) staged."
+    for v in "${violations[@]}"; do
+        red "    - $v"
+    done
+    yel "If a finding is a false positive, run:"
+    yel "    git commit --no-verify"
+    yel "But review the file first — the last leak was a real Ed25519 key."
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+exit 0

@@ -227,6 +227,24 @@ out/
 signing-*.key
 signing-*.pub
 
+# Generic private-key / secret patterns — belt-and-suspenders.
+# A real Ed25519 signing key was committed once via `git add -f` despite
+# signing-*.key being above; .githooks/pre-commit blocks the same mistake at
+# the index level, and these patterns block at the working-tree level.
+*.key
+!*.pub.key
+*.priv
+*.pem
+*.pfx
+*.p12
+id_rsa
+id_dsa
+id_ecdsa
+id_ed25519
+secrets.*
+*-secret
+*-secret.*
+
 # IDE settings
 .vscode/
 .idea/

@@ -111,6 +111,17 @@ Macros vs granular tools
   - `mcp-agent-mail guard status .`
   - `mcp-agent-mail guard install <project_key> . --prepush`
   - Guards exit early when `WORKTREES_ENABLED=0` or `AGENT_MAIL_BYPASS=1`.
+- Install the secrets pre-commit guard (one-time per clone):
+  - `git config core.hooksPath .githooks`
+  - `.githooks/pre-commit` rejects staged files that look like private keys
+    (`*.key`, `*.priv`, `*.pem`, `id_rsa`, `signing-*` without `.pub`, etc.)
+    and blob bodies that contain provider-token shapes
+    (`sk-...`, `ghp_...`, `AKIA...`, PEM `-----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY-----`, etc.)
+  - This is a second layer over `.gitignore`: the gitignore stops innocent
+    `git add`, the hook stops `git add -f`. Both layers are needed because
+    a real Ed25519 signing key was once committed despite `signing-*.key`
+    being in `.gitignore`.
+  - Bypass for genuine false positives: `git commit --no-verify`.
 - Composition details:
   - Installer writes a Python chain-runner to `.git/hooks/pre-commit` and `.git/hooks/pre-push` that executes `hooks.d/<hook>/*` and then `<hook>.orig` if present.
   - Agent Mail guard is installed as `hooks.d/pre-commit/50-agent-mail.py` and `hooks.d/pre-push/50-agent-mail.py`.
